course_title: Special Topics MESAS-200 Level: The New Jew
subject: MESAS
course_description: Selected topics in Economics. May be repeated for credit when topic varies.
course_notes: (Same as DANC 385 1) This course, co-taught by professors in Economics and Dance and Movement Studies, introduces students to the economics of the performing arts. Students will explore live performance as an investment; creative production, performances, audience demand and prestige; how public and nonprofit support are shaped by markets and value systems; contracting and incentives for performers and producers; the role of digital platforms in streaming, ticketing, and promotion; property rights and royalties, as well as labor markets for artists and unions. Case studies, drawn from non-profit and profitable models, anchor theory in real-world applications. Performing Arts students missing ECON 101 and 112, please contact Erica Bowersox (erica.bowersox@emory.edu) for enrollment.
